kept.text:
$SOFI Tech platform thesis is dead for 2026 I guess?... 

CFO on Q1 Earnings: "we expect our tech platform net revenue to be approximately $325 M for the full year."

Q1 tech revenue = $75 M

So, $250 mil. is the guide for the remaining 3 quarters.

250/3 = $83.3 mil per quarter. 

Meaning, if tech platform makes sub 100M per quarter from now on, SoFi will still hit guide for the year.

kept.text:
$sofi

Ok I submitted the song I wrote for the Sofi contest and the other required video and essay. 

The song is 
The American Way.

$SOFI is barely over its IPO price 11$. Let that sink in. Profitable, Bank Charter, million member quarters, accelerating growth, massive unaided brand awareness ticking up, but because of some taxes and an underperforming tech platform, the business is being painted as dead by bears. 

This too shall pass. It won’t stay down forever, and I imagine in the back half of this year we’ll see incredible growth in EPS as well as the other metrics. 

If you have been a shareholder for as long as I have, we’ve been here before.

Since I've been around $SoFi I watched it get pushed down to $4, then down to $6, then last year to $8. 

If the trend holds do they get it to $10? Alternative theory, last year they essentially pushed it to 2X TBV. If thats the case is $14-15 a floor? Time will tell. 🤷🏼‍♂️

tweet.text:
Ugly day for two big retail names, $SOFI & $HOOD.

Amazing day for the AI Infra plays that continue to crush earnings like $BE $STX $NXP.

I think the broader question here, at this stage of the rally from the lows, is do you think there is an opportunity cost at being in a loser and transitioning it for a winner.

The loser, if the company is doing well, usually gets the dip buy because the price is a better bargain.

This market is rewarding the winner, even if the earnings multiple is massive (like Intel) or there barely are any earnings (like Bloom) as long as the trajectory continues to show that they will keep winning.

So, would you sell all your $SOFI to buy a $SNDK or $MU here?   

I’m not sure many would, but so far fintech and software have continued to dip even with good earnings as the market simply has rotated to companies that keep putting up strong numbers.

Just not sure many have the stomach to buy a chart up 3-4x YTD and sell their bags in fundamentally strong companies just to be in the current winning trade.
